root_causes.cause: R2 is not advertising the 172.16.0.0/24 LAN into OSPF, so upstream routers (R1) do not have a route to 172.16.0.11 and cannot forward the pings.
evidence: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 configured with IP 172.16.0.1/24 (get_running_config: R2).
R2 OSPF process-1 network statements show only 10.0.0.0 and 10.0.0.4 (no 172.16.0.0) (get_running_config: R2).
R1 OSPF process-1 network statements include 192.168.0.0 but do not include 172.16.0.0 (get_running_config: R1).
R1 has no routed default (
routing": null) to reach unknown destinations, so absent an OSPF advertisement R1 will not forward to 172.16.0.0 (get_running_config: R1).

data.intent_description: R2 is not advertising the 172.16.0.0/24 LAN into OSPF, preventing R1 and upstream routers from reaching 172.16.0.11; restore OSPF advertisement so traffic to 172.16.0.0/24 is routable.

plan.problem: R2 OSPF process-1 lacks a network statement for 172.16.0.0/24 (interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0 is 172.16.0.1/24), so the prefix is not advertised and R1 has no route to 172.16.0.11.
plan.fix_summary: Add 172.16.0.0/24 to R2 OSPF process-1 network statements (advertise via GigabitEthernet0/0/0 into the correct area) so upstream routers receive the route.

messages.content:
What I observed
- R2's LAN (172.16.0.0/24) was configured on GigabitEthernet0/0/0 but not advertised in OSPF, so R1 and upstream routers had no route to 172.16.0.11.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- Missing OSPF network statement on R2 for 172.16.0.0/24 (OSPF process 1), so the prefix was not being flooded into area 0.

Change made
- Added OSPF network statement on R2: process 1, network 172.16.0.0 wildcard 0.0.0.255 area 0 (advertises Gi0/0/0).

Verification
- Passed: Ping from R1 to 172.16.0.11 now succeeds (4/5 replies, min/avg/max 3/3/4 ms). R2 Gi0/0/0 is administratively and operationally up.
- Remaining: OSPF shows Gi0/0/0 interface_state "Waiting" (adjacency/advertisement for the LAN may not be fully established). Also observed intermittent packet loss (1/5 pings lost) — recommend investigating LAN host(s) or link errors if reliability is required.

data.last_message:
What I observed
- R2's LAN (172.16.0.0/24) was configured on GigabitEthernet0/0/0 but not advertised in OSPF, so R1 and upstream routers had no route to 172.16.0.11.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- Missing OSPF network statement on R2 for 172.16.0.0/24 (OSPF process 1), so the prefix was not being flooded into area 0.

Change made
- Added OSPF network statement on R2: process 1, network 172.16.0.0 wildcard 0.0.0.255 area 0 (advertises Gi0/0/0).

Verification
- Passed: Ping from R1 to 172.16.0.11 now succeeds (4/5 replies, min/avg/max 3/3/4 ms). R2 Gi0/0/0 is administratively and operationally up.
- Remaining: OSPF shows Gi0/0/0 interface_state "Waiting" (adjacency/advertisement for the LAN may not be fully established). Also observed intermittent packet loss (1/5 pings lost) — recommend investigating LAN host(s) or link errors if reliability is required.
